analytical_depth.rationale: Exceptional depth. The adjustments for IndAS 116 to normalize margins and the insight that ROE expansion was driven by buyback-induced equity compression rather than operating leverage are institutional-grade.

logical_consistency.rationale: Highly consistent. The narrative seamlessly connects the balance sheet expansion (intangibles/gross block) to the ROE dip and cash flow compression, drawing logical, evidence-backed conclusions.

completeness.rationale: Covers almost all critical dimensions comprehensively. The only minor gap is the missing full-year constant currency growth and utilization rate, which are acknowledged as data limitations.

actionability.rationale: Provides clear watch items (data center capex, M&A intangibles, DSO) and a forward view, but lacks a strictly formatted Bull/Bear framework to weigh these scenarios.

sector_framework.rationale: Perfect application of IT services frameworks. Analyzes employee cost creep, subcontracting levers, TCV, attrition, and milestone-based billing impacts on DSO.

data_sourcing.rationale: Impeccable data sourcing. Every claim is backed by cited tool data, calculations are explicitly shown, and concall data caveats are prominently displayed.

tool_use_discipline.rationale: Execution log shows textbook discipline: used TOCs before drilling down, utilized the calculate tool for all math, and honestly surfaced compliance gate failures.

cost_efficiency.rationale: At $0.82 and 39 turns, the execution is highly inefficient. The turn count (>20) borders on an F, but it is graded C due to the sub-$1.00 cost and strong 79% cache hit rate.

issues.issue: The report lacks a structured Bull/Bear framework, despite providing good forward-looking commentary and a sensitivity matrix mention.
issues.suggestion: Update instructions to explicitly require a formatted Bull/Bear case scenario with clear conditional triggers for the stock.

issues.issue: Constant currency revenue growth for FY26 and utilization rates were not successfully extracted.
issues.suggestion: Enhance the extraction pipeline to pull full-year CC growth from annual press releases if concalls are unavailable, and verify utilization rate extraction logic for IT services.

strengths: Brilliant normalization of historical margins by adjusting for IndAS 116 lease accounting distortions.
Nuanced DuPont analysis correctly identifying that ROE expansion was driven by buybacks (equity compression) rather than debt leverage or margin expansion.
it_services.summary: This is an institutional-quality financial deep-dive that excels in analytical depth, particularly in its treatment of IndAS 116 and DuPont mechanics. The primary drawback is the excessive agent looping (39 turns) required to produce it, and the slight omission of a formalized Bull/Bear framework.
